channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah fires about 250 rockets and other projectiles into Israel in heaviest barrage in weeks
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired about 250 rockets and other projectiles into Israel on Sunday, wounding seven people in one of the militant group's heaviest barrages in months, in response to deadly Israeli strikes in Beirut while negotiators pressed on with cease-fire efforts to halt the all-out war.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How can you deal with politics at Thanksgiving this year? Stay CALM
item.description: There are strategies people can take to prepare for political conflict this holiday season, Dr. Mena Mirhom says.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ingle, NewsNation

item.title: ATV operator dies after being run over by ambulance in Missouri
item.description: The incident happened early Sunday morning in Kansas City, Missouri after a man driving an ATV crashed into the ambulance and fell into its path.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: New details emerge on BioLab fire that forced thousands to shelter outside Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Federal authorities have released an update on the investigation into fires at the BioLab chemical plant near Atlanta that produced a toxic chemical cloud and forced nearby residents to shelter in place. The fires broke out Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Art exhibit at Tennessee university museum draws controversy
item.description: The artist said the piece is a response to fascism, which features a swastika morphing into a cross behind
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Hagood, NewsNation

item.title: You can get paid $2,500 to shop at Costco for a week
item.description: Attention Costco shoppers! How would you like to get paid $2,500 plus a free Costco membership to shop at your favorite store?
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Salad topping sold in Florida, 21 other states recalled over wrong ingredient
item.description: The packagers should contain tortilla strips but instead, consumers have found crispy onion strips inside.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chuck Woolery, smooth-talking game show host of 'Love Connection' and 'Scrabble,' dies at 83
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chuck Woolery, the affable, smooth-talking game show host of “Wheel of Fortune,” “Love Connection” and “Scrabble” who later became a right-wing podcaster, skewering liberals and accusing the government of lying about COVID-19, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chuck Woolery, former host of 'Wheel of Fortune' and more, dies
item.description: Chuck Woolery, the affable, smooth-talking game show host of “Wheel of Fortune,” “Love Connection” and “Scrabble” who later became a right-wing podcaster, skewering liberals and accusing the government of lying about COVID-19, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of Pennsylvania teen at center of 1973 cold case identified as investigation continues
item.description: Ruth Elizabeth Brenneman was last seen leaving for school more than 50 years ago. Now, her remains have been identified as the investigation into her disappearance continues.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salad topping sold in 22 states recalled over wrong ingredient
item.description: The packages should contain tortilla strips but instead, consumers have found crispy onion strips inside.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: After Trump's win, Black women are rethinking their role as America's reliable political organizers
item.description: Donald Trump's victory has dismayed many politically engaged Black women, and they're reassessing their enthusiasm for politics and organizing.
item.dc:creator: Kenya Hunter, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The rising price of paying the national debt is a risk for Trump's promises on growth and inflation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has big plans for the economy — and a big debt problem that will be a hurdle to delivering on them.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel cracks down on Palestinian citizens who speak out against the war in Gaza
item.description: UMM AL-FAHM, Israel (AP) — Israel’s yearlong crackdown against Palestinian citizens who speak out against the war in Gaza is prompting many to self-censor out of fear of being jailed and further marginalized in society, while some still find ways to dissent — carefully.
item.dc:creator: SAM MCNEIL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Glen Powell to star in latest hit: a look-alike competition
item.description: After an appearance in "Top Gun: Maverick" and leading roles in "Anyone but You," "Hitman" and "Twisters," Austin native and Hollywood A-lister Glen Powell is set to star in his latest smash hit: A look-alike competition.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps NFL player Scott Turner as head of HUD
item.description: Turner serves as the chair of the Center for Education Opportunity at America First Policy Institute, a Trump-aligned think tank.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: NATO head and Trump meet in Florida for talks on global security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump and the head of NATO have met for talks on global security, the military alliance said Saturday. In a brief statement, NATO said Trump and its secretary general, Mark Rutte, met on Friday in Palm Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Driver in crash that killed 4 kids was allegedly photographing ‘Welcome to Florida’ sign
item.description: A New York man was extradited to Florida this month after he was accused of causing a deadly crash when he came to a near-stop on Interstate 95 to take photo of the "Welcome to Florida" sign.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9

item.title: Jelly Roll pays tribute to Oklahoma teen who died from fentanyl
item.description: The 19-year-old died from a fentanyl overdose in May 2023, while attending Cameron University. Rain, who thought she was taking a Xanax, turned out the pill was laced with fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Mario Gonzalez/KFOR, KRON4

item.title: Family hoping for justice after podcast covers decades-old central Ohio cold case
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) – A podcaster has wrapped up her investigation into a decades-old central Ohio cold case, giving the victim’s family hope of finally receiving justice. John “Bernie” Burnside, 32, was found dead in his Marysville home on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for a coyote in Massachusetts town. The owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident that has the animal's owner fuming, but that local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 convicted in human smuggling case after Indian family froze to death on US-Canada border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A jury convicted two men on Friday of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S. border during a 2022 blizzard.
item.dc:creator: MARK VANCLEAVE and MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Youngkin announces order to protect children from social media
item.description: Governor Glenn Youngkin issued an executive order on Thursday in the hopes of protecting children from addictive social media platforms.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: Teen suspect arrested after cheerleader found dead in woods
item.description: Tennessee cheerleader Savannah Copeland, 13, was allegedly stabbed to death by a 15-year-old after the pair met up around midnight Oct. 22.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah city among the 10 'least sinful' cities in the United States
item.description: Denver was ranked as the No. 6 most "sinful" city in the U.S., ahead of cities like New York City and Miami in a new ranking released by WalletHub.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown: Denaturalization just a drop in the bucket
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming administration is expected to take aim at legal immigration, in addition to cracking down on the illegal variety, slowing the pace of application approvals and redirecting resources to look for fraud in old applications, including naturalizations.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KRON4

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US: Here's where it's worst
item.description: In October, the CDC wrote that since late spring, the number of infections caused by walking pneumonia has been increasing, especially among young children.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, KRON4

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal shifts spotlight to Hegseth, Gabbard, Kennedy
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz's (R-Fla.) decision to withdraw from consideration to become attorney general has shifted the attention of Senate Republicans to the nominations of Pete Hegseth, former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9
